title: Absolute measurement of penetration depth of superconducting thin films using microwave stripline resonators
authors: Arghya Dutta, Ajeet Salunke, Mahesh Poojary, Vivas Bagwe, Sangita Bose, Pratap Raychaudhuri
abstract: Superconducting microstrip resonators, which leverage kinetic inductance to probe electrodynamics, are sensitive tools for studying superconducting thin films at microwave frequencies. However, extracting the absolute superconducting penetration depth from these measurements remains challenging. In this work, we present a hybrid method to determine the absolute value of penetration depth over a wide temperature range by combining resonator measurements with finite-element electromagnetic simulations in COMSOL Multiphysics. We demonstrate this approach by extracting the penetration depth of NbN films by fabricating resonators from films of various thicknesses. Furthermore, we extend the technique to materials with lower critical temperatures by employing a flip-film geometry. By placing a sample above a NbN resonator, separated by a thin Mylar dielectric, we create a coupled structure where changes in the sample's penetration depth shift the resonant frequency. This non-destructive method provides a reliable, high-sensitivity platform for characterizing the penetration depth of diverse superconducting thin films.
summary: This paper presents a new hybrid method to precisely measure the absolute superconducting penetration depth in thin films. By combining microwave resonator experiments with 3D electromagnetic simulations, the authors overcome the limitations of traditional model-dependent analytical methods. The technique is successfully demonstrated on both NbN and a-Re6Zr films, including very thin films using a unique flip-film geometry.
main_problem: The difficulty of determining the absolute value of the superconducting magnetic penetration depth (lambda) from microwave resonator measurements due to model dependencies and complex geometric factors.
main_result: The development of a hybrid method combining microwave measurements with COMSOL Multiphysics simulations to accurately extract absolute penetration depth and superconducting gap values for NbN and a-Re6Zr films.
method: A hybrid approach using finite-element electromagnetic simulations (COMSOL) to create look-up tables of resonant frequencies, which are then matched to experimental transmission (S12) data from microstrip resonators.
model_or_system: Superconducting microstrip (stripline) resonators made of NbN and Nb3Sn thin films on MgO substrates, including a 'flip-film' geometry where a secondary sample is placed above the resonator using a Mylar dielectric spacer.
key_observables: Resonant frequency (fn), transmission coefficient (|S12|), phase shift, and loaded quality factor (QL).
important_parameters: Magnetic penetration depth (lambda), kinetic inductance (Lk), superconducting energy gap (Delta), film thickness (t), and temperature (T).
main_assumptions: Gold-plated copper box surfaces are treated as perfect conductors; the thickness and penetration depth of the primary microstrip are assumed to be fixed for certain calculations; neglect of temperature variation of the resonator lambda in specific variants.
figures_summary: Fig 1: Device geometry and experimental setup (bare and flip-film). Fig 2: Transmission spectra, frequency vs mode number, and temperature dependence of 1/lambda^2 for NbN. Fig 3: Frequency shifts in flip-film configuration and simulated frequency vs lambda. Fig 4: Transmission and extracted lambda for 6 nm NbN. Fig 5/6: Simulated transmission and frequency dependence on conductivity and thickness.
paper_structure: Introduction of the problem (absolute lambda measurement) -> Description of the microstrip resonator and flip-film experimental setup -> Presentation of the hybrid simulation-measurement method -> Validation using NbN films -> Extension and application to a-Re6Zr via flip-film geometry -> Discussion of thickness regimes and error analysis.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
